world_database.culture_note: 28 cultures derived from the 9 eras of Leonorian history. Each culture belongs to a biome, has a dominant race, a temperament (connected to Birth Mother Signs), and a primary Materium school that shapes their magic tradition.

cultures.name: Kesh-Amun
cultures.description: A civilization of 'Measure and Law'. They view the world as a geometric puzzle to be solved. Bureaucracy is their art form and their religion is pragmatism.
cultures.architecture: Stone-block grid cities with precise right-angled streets. Tall record-houses at every intersection.

character_names.surname_convention: Family name from trade registry. Format: [family] of [district]. E.g. 'Marat of the Scales'.

cultures.name: Oru-Vat
cultures.description: River-folk who use Vitalism to filter the 'Death-Silt' — contamination from the Badlands that washes downstream — from their water. Deeply communal. A culture where no one eats until everyone eats.
cultures.stance: Cooperative, pacifist by preference, defensive when pushed
cultures.architecture: Floating platforms and raised stilted villages along river edges. Living roofs of cultivated reeds.

character_names.surname_convention: Named after the river they were born nearest to.

cultures.name: Thul-Gaur
cultures.description: A culture defined by the 'Shield-Wall' mentality. Defensive architecture is their art form. Every farmhouse can become a fortress in an afternoon. They guard others as a matter of identity.
cultures.architecture: Low stone fortifications everywhere. Even markets have defensive walls. Gate-culture — all entrances are treated as security points.

character_names.surname_convention: Clan name tied to a defensive structure or battle won.

cultures.name: Hath-Vek
cultures.description: A hierarchical caste system where 'Will' is the only currency. Power is treated as a physical weight — the strong carry it, the weak are crushed by it. An efficient, brutal society.
cultures.stance: Authoritarian, expansionist, culturally contemptuous of weakness
cultures.architecture: Tall, stark towers. Public displays of strength (arenas, public judgments). No ornamentation — efficiency over beauty.

character_names.surname_convention: Name of the rank or title held. Changes when status changes.

cultures.name: Zher-Ith
cultures.description: Survivalist heat-shapers. They see the desert not as a home, but as a testing ground. Trade is their lifeblood — the canyon routes are the only viable paths through the Badlands, and Zher-Ith controls them.
cultures.architecture: Cut into canyon walls. Heat-dispersing architecture. Nothing is built at ground level.

character_names.surname_convention: Canyon name they were born in, or trade route they operate.

cultures.name: Skaal-Vek
cultures.description: They practice sensory deprivation and emotional stillness to avoid attracting the forest horrors. The Skaal believe that emotion is a signal — and the forest listens for signals.
cultures.architecture: Invisible settlements — buildings designed to blend with trees. No fires visible from outside. Sound-dampening materials everywhere.

character_names.surname_convention: None publicly. Single-name culture. Secret family names used only within the household.

cultures.name: Ix-Inar
cultures.description: A culture of kinetic art and silk-trade. They weaponize luxury and social influence. Their markets are the most beautiful in Leonoria, and their diplomats are the most dangerous.
cultures.architecture: Open-air pavilion markets. Silk awnings everywhere. Buildings look soft even when they are stone.

character_names.surname_convention: League house affiliation. Format: [house name]-kin. E.g. 'Silk-kin'.

cultures.name: Uru-Kesh
cultures.description: Iconoclasts who specialize in unmaking 'Unjust Oaths' and breaking spiritual bindings. They are enemies of the Hath-Vek and everything the Kesh Hegemony represents in its worst form.
cultures.architecture: Improvised but sturdy. Freehold clusters that can be abandoned and rebuilt quickly.

character_names.surname_convention: No surnames — names the Freeholds reject hierarchy implicitly.
examples: (first name only, or add 'Free' prefix: Freeborn Harad)

cultures.name: Makh-Zai
cultures.description: Scavengers who believe that only in the breaking of things is the Materium truly freed. They pick through the ruins of the Great Death and believe the cataclysm was a holy act of liberation.
cultures.architecture: Built from ruins. Nothing is purpose-built — everything is reassembled wreckage.

character_names.surname_convention: Name of a ruin they were born in or claim.

cultures.name: Nor-Kindu
cultures.description: Hardened miners and hunters whose blood has partially crystalized from Norium exposure. They keep a 'Vigil' — a permanent watch against the deep cold and its undead. The last warm lights on the edge of the world.
cultures.architecture: Deep-cut stone halls underground with minimal surface exposure. Heat retention is the primary architectural goal.

character_names.surname_convention: Mine or vein they were born in or work. Format: [metal]-born.

cultures.name: Qar-Mal
cultures.description: Nomads who use polished Materium glass to blind enemies across the flat plains. Their name means 'Those who use the light as a blade'. Exceptional mounted warriors.
cultures.architecture: No permanent structures. Portable hide-and-bone camps. Pack and move in half a day.

character_names.surname_convention: Herd name. The herd they were born into is their family name.

cultures.name: Vahn-Ree
cultures.description: Sea-reivers who call the 'Black Mist' to hide their fleet during the Gleam-tide. Traders and pirates depending on who is asking. Masters of the coastal waters.
cultures.stance: Maritime-dominant, commercially ruthless, pleasurable when profitable
cultures.architecture: Low coastal forts and deep natural harbors with hidden anchorage.

character_names.surname_convention: Ship name. Format: [Ship] captain or crew. 'Mist of the Black Dawn'.

cultures.name: Xul-Dath
cultures.description: Cursed wanderers who claim the Great Death was a holy event that must be finished. They move through the ruins of the Blinding Lands, completing what the cataclysm started.
cultures.stance: Apocalyptic, nihilistic, deeply feared by neighbouring cultures
cultures.architecture: Ruins repurposed as temples to destruction.

character_names.surname_convention: None. The Xul-Dath believe names are possessions and possessions are chains.

cultures.name: Bas-Tuut
cultures.description: Bio-diplomats who negotiate through pheromones and pulse-manipulation. Their 'diplomacy' includes subtle physical influence on emotional states. They are excellent trade partners and deeply unsettling negotiating opponents.
cultures.architecture: Living caravans — everything is mobile, but the caravans are built like small cities.

character_names.surname_convention: Caravan lineage name. Format: Caravan [name].

cultures.name: Dra-Vosh
cultures.description: They do not speak; they pulse rhythmically with the forest's root system. Communication is physical — touch and vibration. They maintain the psychic equilibrium of the ancient forest.
cultures.architecture: Living trees shaped over generations. Nothing cut, only grown.

character_names.surname_convention: Tree or grove they were born under.

regions: Ancient Forests of the Sanctuary Lands

cultures.name: Ghor-Aum
cultures.description: They build inverted cities that hang from the underside of high mountain cliffs. Their architecture defies reason until you understand their geology. The most technically advanced builders in Leonoria.
cultures.architecture: Inverted — hanging from cliff undersides. Access by rope and pulley. Mind-bending to outsiders.

character_names.surname_convention: Mountain or ridge name. Format: [mountain]-blood.

regions: High Peaks of the Sanctuary Lands

cultures.name: Vun-Dar
cultures.description: Blind, vibration-sensitive miners who map the world through sound. They have never seen sunlight and have no concept of visual art. Their culture is entirely sonic.
cultures.stance: Deeply insular, neutral to outsiders if they are quiet
cultures.architecture: Carved sound-chambers. Every space is designed for specific acoustic properties.

character_names.surname_convention: Sound they were named for at birth.

regions: The Forgotten Kingdom — The Well Zone

cultures.name: Licht-Ka
cultures.description: Translucent-skinned folk who survive on the light emitted from the world-veins — underground channels of residual Materium from the Great Death. The closest thing to a welcoming culture in the Forgotten Kingdom.
cultures.stance: Open to surface contact (the only Forgotten Kingdom culture that is)
cultures.architecture: Crystal-and-light aesthetics. Bioluminescent pathways. Open spaces designed to maximize light spread.

character_names.surname_convention: World-vein they live nearest to.

regions: The Forgotten Kingdom — The Light Zone

cultures.name: Vann-Ok
cultures.description: Their culture views fermentation and decay as the pinnacle of chemistry. Their 'art' is poison and rot. Their medicines cure things that kill other races. Outsiders are terrified of them; they find outsiders baffling.
cultures.stance: Deeply alien to outsiders, internally coherent and skilled
cultures.architecture: Bog platforms with fermentation vats visible everywhere. The smell is part of the culture.

character_names.surname_convention: Compound they specialize in.

cultures.name: Ash-Korr
cultures.description: A society of 'The End'. They use erasure-magic to prune history they find unworthy. They believe they are doing the world a service — removing what should never have been.
cultures.stance: Methodical, cold, absolutely convinced of their own rectitude
cultures.architecture: Purposeful emptiness. Rooms where things have been erased. Architecture of absence.

character_names.surname_convention: What they have erased or plan to erase.

regions: The Forgotten Kingdom — The Fallen Zone

cultures.name: Syl-Uun
cultures.description: Hairy, mossy hunters who don animal hides that eventually fuse to their skin. The deep forest is their entire world — and they defend it absolutely.
cultures.architecture: None — live in the forest itself.

character_names.surname_convention: Animal whose hide they currently wear.

cultures.name: Gor-Vann
cultures.description: Stubborn, root-like recluses who can remain motionless for days in the mud. They have been mistaken for dead trees by every culture that encountered them.
cultures.stance: Immovable, patient, responds only to direct threat
cultures.architecture: None. They are the architecture.

character_names.surname_convention: Bog or thicket they claim.

cultures.name: Erem-Ka
cultures.description: A reclusive 'Garden-Culture'. They cultivate healing spores that act as local medicine. The most approachable of the Wildmen cultures by a significant margin — still antisocial, but will trade healing for being left alone.
cultures.architecture: Spore gardens that triple as homes, medicine supplies, and defensive barriers.

character_names.surname_convention: Spore or plant they specialize in growing.

cultures.name: Grak-Var
cultures.description: 8-foot-tall warriors who demand tribute in the form of 'Will-Aura' and service. Their legions function like a martial empire — organized, terrifying, and perpetually expanding their zone of tribute.
cultures.stance: Expansionist, tribute-collecting, violent enforcer of hierarchy
cultures.architecture: Crude but enormous forts. Every structure is scaled for 8-foot beings.

character_names.surname_convention: Legion number and rank.

cultures.name: Maw-Shulk
cultures.description: Ritualistic predators who believe that eating an enemy's heart transfers their Conviction. Their battles are religious ceremonies. Their meals are theology.
cultures.stance: Ritualistic, predatory, absolutely alien moral framework
cultures.architecture: Bone and hide. Every structure is decorated with the remains of victories.

character_names.surname_convention: Strongest enemy consumed.

cultures.name: Skard-Hul
cultures.description: Unstoppable brutes who use kinetic force to shatter gates and chains. The Horde considers all obstacles — physical and social — as things to be broken through. Ironically, they are one of the few Wildmen cultures that actively trades with outsiders.
cultures.architecture: Mobile siege camps. They bring the walls down and live in them.

character_names.surname_convention: Obstacle broken.

cultures.name: Uun-Dhal
cultures.description: Aloof entities who speak in chord-tones. They maintain the 'Psychic Equilibrium' of the forest — a function they believe is essential to preventing another Great Death in their region.
cultures.stance: Deeply isolationist, maintain the forest as a sacred duty
cultures.architecture: Sound-architecture — structures that produce specific harmonics when wind moves through them.

character_names.surname_convention: Chord-tone name — a specific harmonic frequency rendered phonetically.

regions: The Ancient Deep of the Sanctuary Lands

cultures.name: Grath-Ka
cultures.description: They shape Materium into air-tight, windowless towers. Logic is their primary god. The Grath Spires are the closest thing to formal magical universities in Leonoria.
cultures.architecture: Tall, windowless spires. No ornamentation. Precision construction.

character_names.surname_convention: Discipline or school of study.

cultures.name: Cry-Ath
cultures.description: Beings of living frost. They do not eat; they absorb the conviction of the wind. Their culture is so alien that no outsider has ever been permitted inside — but their Redoubt is visible, a fortress of ice that does not melt.
cultures.stance: Absolutely xenophobic, but passively so — they do not attack unless approached
cultures.architecture: Living ice structures that grow in specific geometric patterns. No joints or seams — grown not built.

character_names.surname_convention: Ice formation they were born within.

cultures.name: Shul-Um
cultures.description: A cabal that rules through 'The Weight of Souls'. They see time as a resource to be spent and have spent millennia building influence networks that extend into the surface world through agents, proxies, and blackmail.
cultures.stance: Expansionist through proxies, manipulative, long-game power players
cultures.architecture: Deep black stone cities that were old when the surface world was young.

character_names.surname_convention: Shadow-name — a name that describes their function in the Syndicate.
